changes: OpenClaw did not post a new release since v2026.4.15, but repo activity stayed hot, with the main repo reaching roughly 359.7k stars by the 2026-04-18 scan.
Hermes Agent had the clearest demand jump today, climbing from roughly 95k to about 97.6k stars by the 2026-04-18 scan while its latest stable remained v2026.4.16.
ZeroClaw kept shipping aggressively with v0.7.0-beta.1040 at 2026-04-17 08:42 UTC, v0.7.0-beta.1041 at 2026-04-17 15:03 UTC, and v0.7.1-beta.1045 at 2026-04-18 07:20 UTC.
SwarmClaw continued its control-plane sprint with v1.5.64, v1.5.65, and v1.5.66 all published between 2026-04-17 21:07 UTC and 2026-04-17 21:44 UTC.
PicoClaw posted another nightly build at 2026-04-18 00:58 UTC, keeping the edge-device lane active without a new stable cut.
whyItMatters: OpenClaw is still the anchor, but today’s strongest signal was not an anchor release, it was ecosystem follow-on activity around it.
Hermes is compounding into a serious adjacent benchmark because it is pairing learning-loop positioning with visible adoption momentum.
ZeroClaw is reinforcing the lightweight Rust lane as a fast-moving branch with near-continuous prerelease iteration.
SwarmClaw keeps validating the operator/control-plane layer as a real category branch, not just an experiment.
PicoClaw still supports the cheap-hardware thesis, though today was another cadence signal rather than a strategic step-change.
